title: Language Body Sections
description: Markdown body sections expected in every language file. Not frontmatter — extracted by ingest, never validated by JSON Schema.

## Overview.description: One-to-two sentence description of the language and its primary use case. Write once on creation; do not overwrite.

## Strengths.description: Bullet list of strengths noted by the user. Only populated from explicit user opinion, never inferred. Append new bullets; never duplicate.

## Weaknesses.description: Bullet list of weaknesses noted by the user. Only populated from explicit user opinion, never inferred. Append new bullets; never duplicate.

## Notes.description: Free-form observations recorded verbatim from the user. Append paragraphs; preserve existing text.

## Data Types.description: Primitive and object types with mutability noted. Append new types; never duplicate.

name.description: Type name, e.g. int, str, list

## Operators.description: Language operators with symbol, name, category, and a usage example. Append; never duplicate.

type.description: Category, e.g. arithmetic, comparison, logical, assignment

## Comment Types.description: Comment syntaxes supported by the language. Append; never duplicate.

## Trick Questions.description: Gotchas, surprising behaviors, and common mistakes. Append new items; never duplicate.

## Built-ins.description: Standard library / built-in capabilities — maps a concept to the function or namespace that provides it. Append; never duplicate.

concept.description: Capability name, e.g. temp_file_creation, sorted_map, regex
